name: Adamantite Sword
description: The Adamantite Sword is a Hardmode broadsword. Its alternate ore counterpart is the Titanium Sword.

name: Ash Wood Sword
description: The Ash Wood Sword is a pre-Hardmode broadsword and a variation of the Wooden Sword. Its best modifier is Legendary.

name: Bat Bat
description: The Bat Bat is a pre-Hardmode broadsword that has a 1/300 (0.33%) chance of being dropped by any type of bat. When swung, it restores 1 health to the user upon hitting an enemy.[1] On The Constant seed, the drop chance is increased to a 1/100 (1%) chance.

stats.Tooltip: Heals user slightly on hit'Maybe I could bat some bats with this.'

name: Beam Sword
description: The Beam Sword is a Hardmode broadsword that has a 1/150 (0.67%) chance of being dropped by Armored Skeletons. When swung, it fires a yellow Sword Beam projectile that can pierce one enemy or bounce off blocks three times before dissipating. Its best modifier is Legendary.

stats.Tooltip: Shoots a beam of light

name: Bee Keeper
description: The Bee Keeper is a pre-Hardmode broadsword that has a 1/3 (33.33%) chance of being dropped by the Queen Bee. When swung, it will inflict the Confused debuff on hit for 2 seconds.[1] It also summons a burst of 1–3 Bee projectiles on hit, which home in on enemies, deal 1/3 (33.33%) of the weapon's damage (10 base damage), can pierce enemies twice and bounce on blocks three times, and have a 1/2 (50%) to deal 1 extra damage. Its best modifier is Legendary.

stats.Tooltip: Summons killer bees after striking your foeCauses confusion

name: Blade of Grass
description: The Blade of Grass is a pre-Hardmode broadsword. When swung, it fires a Leaf Blade projectile that travels upwards in a circle and homes in on enemies. The Leaf Blade projectile can pass through blocks, deals 25% of the weapon's damage (4 base damage), has 20 armor penetration, and can pierce two enemies.[1] The sword and its projectiles both have a 1/4 (25%) chance of inflicting the Poisoned debuff for 7 seconds upon hit.[2] Its best modifier is Legendary.

stats.Tooltip: Has a chance to poison enemies

name: Bladed Glove
description: The Bladed Glove is a pre-Hardmode broadsword that has a 1/2000 (0.05%) chance of being dropped by any weak enemy during the Halloween seasonal event. It has a very short range, but an exceptionally high attack speed. Its best modifier is Legendary.

name: Bladetongue
description: The Bladetongue is a Hardmode broadsword that can be obtained extremely rarely by fishing in the Crimson biome in any layer. On enemy hits, it inflicts the Ichor debuff for 5–9 seconds and fires an ichor stream at a random angle in front of the player. The ichor stream deals 70% of the weapon's damage and knockback (38 base damage and a knockback of 4.025 (Average)),[1] pierces 4 enemies, and also inflicts Ichor for 3–7 seconds. The ichor stream can bounce on blocks up to 3 times and disappears either on hitting the fourth block or after approximately 2 seconds.

stats.Tooltip: Spits an Ichor stream on contact

name: Blood Butcherer
description: The Blood Butcherer is a pre-Hardmode broadsword. When swung, it inflicts the Blood Butchered debuff upon enemy hits, dealing 4 damage per second for 9 seconds and causing enemies to visibly bleed. Repeated attacks will cause the debuff to stack up to five times, for a total of 20 damage per second.[1] It is the Crimson counterpart to The Corruption's Light's Bane. The Blood Butcherer has a greater size and base damage than the Light's Bane, but has a slower speed.

stats.Debuff: Blood Butchered(Desktop, Console and Mobile versions)

name: Blue Phaseblade
description: Phaseblades are a group of pre-Hardmode broadswords. There are nine different color variations with identical statistics. All phaseblades emit a small amount of light when swung. Letting go of ⚒ Use / Attack while swinging will throw the weapons, causing them to sharply drop down after travelling for 0.5 seconds[1] or upon contact with blocks, then embed themselves in the ground and continuously damage any enemy that touches them. They can be recalled by pressing ⚒ Use / Attack again, which will damage enemies along the way. They can also be recalled mid-throw. Phasesabers are the Hardmode upgrades of the phaseblades.

name: Blue Phasesaber
description: Phasesabers are a group of Hardmode broadswords, direct upgrades to the phaseblades with much more damage and slightly faster use time. There are nine different color variations with identical statistics. All phasesabers emit a small amount of light when swung. Letting go of ⚒ Use / Attack while swinging will throw the weapons, causing them to sharply drop down after travelling for 0.5 seconds[1] or upon contact with blocks, then embed themselves in the ground and continuously damage any enemy that touches them. They can be recalled by pressing ⚒ Use / Attack again, which will damage enemies along the way. They can also be recalled mid-throw. Their best modifier is Legendary.

name: Bone Sword
description: The Bone Sword is a pre-Hardmode broadsword that has a 1/200 (0.5%) chance of being dropped by Skeletons and Spore Skeletons. Upon hitting an enemy, it releases a non-piercing lobbed bone projectile that deals 75% of the weapon's damage (14 base damage) and its full knockback.[1] Its best modifier is Legendary.

name: Boreal Wood Sword
description: The Boreal Wood Sword is a pre-Hardmode broadsword and a variation of the Wooden Sword. Its best modifier is Legendary.

name: Brand of the Inferno
description: The Brand of the Inferno is a Hardmode, post-one mechanical boss broadsword. It has a 1/4 (25%) chance of inflicting the Hellfire debuff for five seconds on hit and also allows the player to "parry" incoming damage while equipped with a shield. While holding the Brand of the Inferno with any equipped shield (excluding the Huntress's Buckler), pressing the ⚷ Open / Activate button will cause the player to raise the shield. When timed right before taking a hit, the enemy's attack will be parried, which negates the contact damage being dealt to the player, deals 200% of the contact damage of the attacking enemy, inflicts a knockback value of 5 (Average), and grants the player 30 invincibility frames (60 with the Cross Necklace equipped).[1] Successfully parrying an attack also grants the Striking Moment buff, causing the next melee-based attack to deal 500% or five times its usual base damage for the next five seconds. Alternatively, if the shield is held in place, 20 defense is temporarily gained; this lasts until the shield is lowered.

stats.Tooltip: Right click to guard with a shield
stats.Buff: Striking Moment(Desktop, Console and Mobile versions)
stats.Buff tooltip: 400% increased damage for next melee strike

name: Breaker Blade
description: The Breaker Blade is a Hardmode broadsword that has a 1/4 (25%) chance of being dropped by the Wall of Flesh. It has a larger size than most other swords. It deals 250% damage (175 base damage) to enemies over 90% health.[1] Its best modifier is Legendary.

stats.Tooltip: Deals more damage to unhurt enemies

name: Breathing Reed
description: The Breathing Reed is a tool that aids in underwater breathing, and also functions as a broadsword. It has a 9/40 (22.5%) chance of being found in Water Chests, Ocean Crates, and Seaside Crates. When selected, the Breathing Reed is held above the player. This slows down the rate at which their breath meter depletes, and halves the rate at which they lose health while drowning. The reed's tip does not need to be above the surface of the water for this to take effect. The player's breath meter refills as soon as the tip of the reed is outside of water, but not within a block.

stats.Tooltip: Increases breath time and allows breathing in water

name: Cactus Sword
description: The Cactus Sword is a pre-Hardmode broadsword. Its best modifier is Legendary.

name: Candy Cane Sword
description: The Candy Cane Sword is a pre-Hardmode broadsword that has a 0.639% (pre-Hardmode) / 0.596% (Hardmode) chance to be obtained from Presents dropped during the Christmas event. Its best modifier is Legendary.

name: Chlorophyte Claymore
description: The Chlorophyte Claymore is a Hardmode, post-mechanical bosses broadsword. When swung, it occasionally fires a Chlorophyte Orb that deals the weapon's damage, can pierce an infinite number of enemies, but cannot pass through blocks. The orb becomes affected by gravity after traveling a short distance. Its best modifier is Legendary.

stats.Tooltip: Shoots a powerful orb

name: Chlorophyte Saber
description: The Chlorophyte Saber is a Hardmode, post-mechanical bosses broadsword. When swung, it occasionally fires a Spore Cloud that deals 70% of the weapon's damage but no knockback, and can pierce an infinite number of enemies. The cloud gradually slows down and fades away. Its best modifier is Legendary.

stats.Tooltip: Shoots a spore cloud

name: Christmas Tree Sword
description: The Christmas Tree Sword is a Hardmode, post-Plantera broadsword that is dropped by the Everscream during the Frost Moon event with a 3.46%–10.37% (6.22%–31.11%) chance depending on the current wave.[1] When swung, it fires a star ornament that releases a ball ornament every 0.17 seconds while traveling. The stars dissipate after piercing one enemy or after 1.67 seconds. The balls linger for 0.83 seconds before falling. Both types of projectiles cannot pass through blocks or enemies. Its best modifier is Legendary.

stats.Tooltip: Shoots Christmas ornaments

name: Classy Cane
description: The Classy Cane is a Hardmode broadsword that has a 1/8 (12.5%) chance to be dropped by the Tax Collector. It will drop 1-25 Copper Coins upon hitting enemies or NPCs. Its best modifier is Legendary.

stats.Tooltip: Enables rapid tax collection

name: Cobalt Sword
description: The Cobalt Sword is a Hardmode broadsword. Its alternate ore counterpart is the Palladium Sword.

name: Copper Broadsword
description: The Copper Broadsword is a pre-Hardmode broadsword. Its alternate ore counterpart is the Tin Broadsword.

name: Cutlass
description: The Cutlass is a Hardmode broadsword that has a 1/200 (0.5%) chance of being dropped by regular Pirate Invasion enemies (excluding the Parrot), a 1/50 (2%) chance from Pirate Captains, and a 1/10 (10%) chance from the Flying Dutchman. Striking enemies with the weapon increases its damage by 12% per hit, up to a maximum of 50%. This damage bonus decays at a rate of 0.5% per tick[1] or 30% per second.[2] Its best modifier is Legendary.

stats.Tooltip: Rapid attacks deal more damage

name: Death Sickle
description: The Death Sickle is a Hardmode broadsword that has a 1/40 (2.5%) / 79/1600 (4.94%) chance of being dropped by Reapers during the Solar Eclipse event. When swung, it fires a spinning purple sickle that can pass through blocks. The sickles travel slowly for about 12 tiles before stopping in place, can pierce four enemies, or linger for three seconds before dissipating. Its best modifier is Legendary.

stats.Tooltip: Shoots a deathly sickle

name: Ebonwood Sword
description: The Ebonwood Sword is a pre-Hardmode broadsword. Its Crimson counterpart is the Shadewood Sword.

name: Enchanted Sword
description: The Enchanted Sword is a pre-Hardmode broadsword. When swung, it occasionally fires an Enchanted Beam projectile that cannot pierce enemies or pass through blocks. Both the sword and its projectile produce lingering particles that emit large amounts of light. The Enchanted Sword can be obtained by destroying an "enchanted sword in stone" background object, which can be found in random locations underground and in Enchanted Sword Shrines. Destroying this background object has a 29/30 (96.67%) chance to drop an Enchanted Sword, with the other 1/30 (3.33%) times instead dropping a Terragrim. The Enchanted Sword also has a 1/30 (3.33%) and 1/15 (6.67%) chance to be found in Golden and Titanium Crates respectively.

stats.Tooltip: Shoots an enchanted sword beam

name: Excalibur
description: The Excalibur is a Hardmode, post-one mechanical boss broadsword. When swung, a large yellow aura follows the swing, reaching about twice the distance of the sword itself. The aura can hit up to three enemies. Its best modifier is Legendary.

name: Exotic Scimitar
description: The Exotic Scimitar is a pre-Hardmode broadsword that has a 1/8 (12.5%) chance of being dropped from the Dye Trader. Striking enemies with the weapon increases its damage by 12% per hit, up to a maximum of 50%. This damage bonus decays at a rate of 0.5% per tick[1] or 30% per second.[2] Its best modifier is Legendary.

stats.Tooltip: Rapid attacks deal more damage

name: Falcon Blade
description: The Falcon Blade is a pre-Hardmode broadsword that has a 38/625 (6.08%) chance of being obtained from Iron Crates and a 299/5000 (5.98%) chance from Mythril Crates. Striking enemies with the weapon increases its damage by 12% per hit, up to a maximum of 50%. This damage bonus decays at a rate of 0.5% per tick[1] or 30% per second.[2] Its best modifier is Legendary.

stats.Tooltip: Rapid attacks deal more damage

name: Fetid Baghnakhs
description: The Fetid Baghnakhs are a Hardmode broadsword that have a 1/5 (20%) chance of being dropped by Crimson Mimics. They have a very short range, but an exceptionally high attack speed. Their best modifier is Legendary.

name: Flying Dragon
description: The Flying Dragon is a Hardmode, post-Golem broadsword that has a 1/4 (25%) chance of being dropped by Betsy during Tier 3 of the Old One's Army event. When swung, it fires red crescent-shaped projectiles every 20 ticks[1][2], which pierce four enemies, pass through blocks, and dissipate after one second. Its best modifier is Legendary.

stats.Tooltip: Unleashes the heart's energy forward

name: Flymeal
description: The Flymeal is a pre-Hardmode broadsword with the unique ability to damage friendly NPCs. It also inflicts the Stinky debuff for 5 seconds, although this effect is purely cosmetic. Its best modifier is Legendary.

stats.Tooltip: Best used for pranking townsfolk'Makes others smell like cilantro'

name: Frostbrand
description: The Frostbrand is a Hardmode broadsword that has a 19/60 (31.67%) chance of being dropped by Ice Mimics. When swung, it fires a Frost Bolt projectile that pierces two enemies, cannot pass through blocks, and inflicts the Frostbite debuff for 4 seconds on hit. Its best modifier is Legendary.

stats.Tooltip: Shoots an icy bolt
stats.Debuff tooltip: It's either really hot or really cold. Either way it REALLY hurts

name: Gold Broadsword
description: The Gold Broadsword is a pre-Hardmode broadsword. Its alternate ore counterpart is the Platinum Broadsword.

name: Green Phaseblade
description: Phaseblades are a group of pre-Hardmode broadswords. There are nine different color variations with identical statistics. All phaseblades emit a small amount of light when swung. Letting go of ⚒ Use / Attack while swinging will throw the weapons, causing them to sharply drop down after travelling for 0.5 seconds[1] or upon contact with blocks, then embed themselves in the ground and continuously damage any enemy that touches them. They can be recalled by pressing ⚒ Use / Attack again, which will damage enemies along the way. They can also be recalled mid-throw. Phasesabers are the Hardmode upgrades of the phaseblades.

name: Green Phasesaber
description: Phasesabers are a group of Hardmode broadswords, direct upgrades to the phaseblades with much more damage and slightly faster use time. There are nine different color variations with identical statistics. All phasesabers emit a small amount of light when swung. Letting go of ⚒ Use / Attack while swinging will throw the weapons, causing them to sharply drop down after travelling for 0.5 seconds[1] or upon contact with blocks, then embed themselves in the ground and continuously damage any enemy that touches them. They can be recalled by pressing ⚒ Use / Attack again, which will damage enemies along the way. They can also be recalled mid-throw. Their best modifier is Legendary.

name: Ham Bat
description: The Ham Bat is a Hardmode broadsword that has a 1/25 (4%) chance of being dropped by Pigrons. It gains slightly increased damage if a food buff is active.[1] Killing an enemy with the Ham Bat grants the Hearty Meal buff, which increases the player's health regeneration by 3 per second.[2] This lasts for 7 seconds, but can be canceled at any time  by right-clicking the icon (),  by selecting the icon using Cycle Next/Previous Buff and then pressing Remove Buff (), or  by double-tapping the buff icon (). The buff will not stack upon killing another enemy; the duration instead resets. In a The Constant world, the drop chance is increased to 1/10 (10%).

stats.Tooltip: Grows more powerful the better fed you areDefeating enemies temporarily improves healing'TIS A WEAPON O' PIG BUTT!'
stats.Buff: Hearty Meal(Desktop, Console and Mobile versions)

name: Ice Blade
description: The Ice Blade is a pre-Hardmode broadsword that is found in Frozen Chests in the Ice biome, Frozen Crates, and Boreal Crates. When swung, it occasionally fires an Ice Bolt projectile that deals the weapon's damage and does not pass through blocks or pierce enemies. The Ice Blade also has a base 6% critical strike chance, as opposed to the usual 4%. Its best modifier is Legendary.

stats.Tooltip: Shoots an icy bolt

name: Ice Sickle
description: The Ice Sickle is a Hardmode broadsword that can be dropped with a 1/100 (1%) chance by Armored Vikings, Ice Elementals, Ice Tortoises, and Icy Mermen. When swung, it fires a spinning blue sickle projectile that deals 67% of the weapon's damage (33 base damage) and can pierce up to 3 enemies. The sickle gradually slows down after being fired, dissipating after traveling for 3 seconds. Its best modifier is Legendary.

stats.Tooltip: Shoots an icy sickle

name: Influx Waver
description: The Influx Waver is a Hardmode, post-Golem broadsword that has a 1/6 (16.67%) chance of being dropped by the Martian Saucer during the Martian Madness event. When swung, it fires a blue sword projectile. Upon contact with enemies, the sword projectile will teleport onto nearby enemies two times, hitting three times in total. Its best modifier is Legendary.

name: Iron Broadsword
description: The Iron Broadsword is a pre-Hardmode broadsword. Its alternate ore counterpart is the Lead Broadsword.

name: Katana
description: The Katana is a pre-Hardmode broadsword that can be purchased from the Traveling Merchant for 5 GC. It has a base 19% critical strike chance, as opposed to the usual 4%. Its best modifier is Legendary.

name: Keybrand
description: The Keybrand is a Hardmode, post-Plantera broadsword that has a 1/200 (0.5%) / 399/40,000 (0.9975%) chance of being dropped by Blue Armored Bones, Hell Armored Bones, and Rusty Armored Bones in the Hardmode Dungeon. It deals increased damage against enemies depending on their missing health, up to a maximum of 200% damage against enemies that have less than 10% health remaining. The Keybrand also has a 20% critical strike chance as opposed to the usual 4%. Letting go of ⚒ Use / Attack while swinging will throw the weapon, causing it to sharply drop down after travelling for 0.5 seconds[1], or touching any solid block, then embed itself in the ground and continuously damage any enemy that touches it. It can be recalled by pressing ⚒ Use / Attack again, which will damage enemies along the way. It can also be recalled mid-throw. Its best modifier is Legendary.

stats.Tooltip: Deals more damage to injured foes

name: Lead Broadsword
description: The Lead Broadsword is a pre-Hardmode broadsword. Its alternate ore counterpart is the Iron Broadsword.

name: Light's Bane
description: The Light's Bane is a pre-Hardmode broadsword. When swung, it creates a dark slash near the tip of the sword. The slashes deal 50% of the weapon's damage (8 base damage),[1] have 5 armor penetration,[2] pierce once, and will automatically target enemies within a radius of 3.125 tiles.[1] The weapon also has a chance to create a big slash that deals 100% of the weapon's damage. The chance of big slashes occurring is equal to the player's melee critical strike chance.[1] It is the Corruption counterpart to The Crimson's Blood Butcherer. The Light's Bane is faster than the Blood Butcherer, but has a lower size and base damage.

name: Mandible Blade
description: The Mandible Blade is a pre-Hardmode broadsword that has a 1/50 (2%) chance of being dropped by Antlion Chargers and Antlion Swarmers. Letting go of ⚒ Use / Attack while swinging will throw the weapon in direction of the cursor, which can travel forward for 0.25 seconds before sharply dropping down. Its best modifier is Legendary.

name: Meowmere
description: The Meowmere is a Hardmode broadsword that has a 1/5 (20%) chance of being dropped by the Moon Lord. When swung, it fires a cat head projectile with a visual rainbow trail every 0.23 seconds. The cat head deals 125% of the weapon's damage (250 base damage), is affected by gravity, and can pierce enemies or bounce off blocks up to four times. Upon bouncing off a block, the cat head makes a "meow" sound and emits damaging rainbow explosions that grow in size with each bounce. Its best modifier is Legendary.

name: Muramasa
description: The Muramasa is a pre-Hardmode, post-Skeletron broadsword that has a 1/7 (14.29%) chance of being obtained from the Locked Gold Chests found in the Dungeon, as well as a 1/7 (14.29%) chance of being obtained from Golden Lock Boxes(Desktop, Console and Mobile versions). When swung, it creates a blue slash upon enemy hits. The slashes deal 50% of the weapon's damage (12 base damage)[1], inflict no knockback, do not pierce, and have 5 armor penetration.[2] Its best modifier is Legendary.

name: Mythril Sword
description: The Mythril Sword is a Hardmode broadsword. Its alternate ore counterpart is the Orichalcum Sword.

name: Night's Edge
description: The Night's Edge is a pre-Hardmode, post-Skeletron broadsword. When swung, a circular purple aura follows the swing, reaching slightly farther than the sword itself. Another aura swings around in front, further increasing range. The auras can hit up to two enemies each, but the same enemy cannot be hit by both auras. Its best modifier is Legendary.

name: Orange Phaseblade
description: Phaseblades are a group of pre-Hardmode broadswords. There are nine different color variations with identical statistics. All phaseblades emit a small amount of light when swung. Letting go of ⚒ Use / Attack while swinging will throw the weapons, causing them to sharply drop down after travelling for 0.5 seconds[1] or upon contact with blocks, then embed themselves in the ground and continuously damage any enemy that touches them. They can be recalled by pressing ⚒ Use / Attack again, which will damage enemies along the way. They can also be recalled mid-throw. Phasesabers are the Hardmode upgrades of the phaseblades.

name: Orange Phasesaber
description: Phasesabers are a group of Hardmode broadswords, direct upgrades to the phaseblades with much more damage and slightly faster use time. There are nine different color variations with identical statistics. All phasesabers emit a small amount of light when swung. Letting go of ⚒ Use / Attack while swinging will throw the weapons, causing them to sharply drop down after travelling for 0.5 seconds[1] or upon contact with blocks, then embed themselves in the ground and continuously damage any enemy that touches them. They can be recalled by pressing ⚒ Use / Attack again, which will damage enemies along the way. They can also be recalled mid-throw. Their best modifier is Legendary.

name: Orichalcum Sword
description: The Orichalcum Sword is a Hardmode broadsword. Its alternate ore counterpart is the Mythril Sword.

name: Palladium Sword
description: The Palladium Sword is a Hardmode broadsword. Its alternate ore counterpart is the Cobalt Sword. The Palladium Sword has slightly higher damage and knockback, but a slower attack speed.

name: Palm Wood Sword
description: The Palm Wood Sword is a pre-Hardmode broadsword and a variation of the Wooden Sword. Its best modifier is Legendary.

name: Pearlwood Sword
description: The Pearlwood Sword is a Hardmode broadsword and a variation of the Wooden Sword. Its best modifier is Legendary.

name: Pink Phaseblade
description: Phaseblades are a group of pre-Hardmode broadswords. There are nine different color variations with identical statistics. All phaseblades emit a small amount of light when swung. Letting go of ⚒ Use / Attack while swinging will throw the weapons, causing them to sharply drop down after travelling for 0.5 seconds[1] or upon contact with blocks, then embed themselves in the ground and continuously damage any enemy that touches them. They can be recalled by pressing ⚒ Use / Attack again, which will damage enemies along the way. They can also be recalled mid-throw. Phasesabers are the Hardmode upgrades of the phaseblades.

name: Pink Phasesaber
description: Phasesabers are a group of Hardmode broadswords, direct upgrades to the phaseblades with much more damage and slightly faster use time. There are nine different color variations with identical statistics. All phasesabers emit a small amount of light when swung. Letting go of ⚒ Use / Attack while swinging will throw the weapons, causing them to sharply drop down after travelling for 0.5 seconds[1] or upon contact with blocks, then embed themselves in the ground and continuously damage any enemy that touches them. They can be recalled by pressing ⚒ Use / Attack again, which will damage enemies along the way. They can also be recalled mid-throw. Their best modifier is Legendary.

name: Platinum Broadsword
description: The Platinum Broadsword is a pre-Hardmode broadsword. Its alternate ore counterpart is the Gold Broadsword.

name: Psycho Knife
description: The Psycho Knife is a Hardmode, post-Plantera broadsword that has a 1/40 (2.5%) / 79/1600 (4.94%) chance of being dropped by Psychos during the Solar Eclipse event. When holding the Psycho Knife and standing still[1], the player will quickly enter stealth, causing them to turn almost invisible, move much more slowly, and no longer be targeted by certain enemies' projectile attacks. Stealth is engaged gradually over the course of 0.83 seconds, during which the player gains several stat boosts that linearly increase to the following values:[2]

stats.Tooltip: Allows you to go into stealth mode

name: Purple Clubberfish
description: The Purple Clubberfish is a pre-Hardmode broadsword that can be obtained by fishing in the Corruption with a 1/10 (10%) chance with 100 Fishing Power, up to a maximum 1/4 (25%) chance with 300 Fishing Power. It has a 1/2 (50%) chance to inflict the Confused debuff on hit. Its best modifier is Legendary.

name: Purple Phaseblade
description: Phaseblades are a group of pre-Hardmode broadswords. There are nine different color variations with identical statistics. All phaseblades emit a small amount of light when swung. Letting go of ⚒ Use / Attack while swinging will throw the weapons, causing them to sharply drop down after travelling for 0.5 seconds[1] or upon contact with blocks, then embed themselves in the ground and continuously damage any enemy that touches them. They can be recalled by pressing ⚒ Use / Attack again, which will damage enemies along the way. They can also be recalled mid-throw. Phasesabers are the Hardmode upgrades of the phaseblades.

name: Purple Phasesaber
description: Phasesabers are a group of Hardmode broadswords, direct upgrades to the phaseblades with much more damage and slightly faster use time. There are nine different color variations with identical statistics. All phasesabers emit a small amount of light when swung. Letting go of ⚒ Use / Attack while swinging will throw the weapons, causing them to sharply drop down after travelling for 0.5 seconds[1] or upon contact with blocks, then embed themselves in the ground and continuously damage any enemy that touches them. They can be recalled by pressing ⚒ Use / Attack again, which will damage enemies along the way. They can also be recalled mid-throw. Their best modifier is Legendary.

name: Rainbow Phaseblade
description: Phaseblades are a group of pre-Hardmode broadswords. There are nine different color variations with identical statistics. All phaseblades emit a small amount of light when swung. Letting go of ⚒ Use / Attack while swinging will throw the weapons, causing them to sharply drop down after travelling for 0.5 seconds[1] or upon contact with blocks, then embed themselves in the ground and continuously damage any enemy that touches them. They can be recalled by pressing ⚒ Use / Attack again, which will damage enemies along the way. They can also be recalled mid-throw. Phasesabers are the Hardmode upgrades of the phaseblades.

name: Rainbow Phasesaber
description: Phasesabers are a group of Hardmode broadswords, direct upgrades to the phaseblades with much more damage and slightly faster use time. There are nine different color variations with identical statistics. All phasesabers emit a small amount of light when swung. Letting go of ⚒ Use / Attack while swinging will throw the weapons, causing them to sharply drop down after travelling for 0.5 seconds[1] or upon contact with blocks, then embed themselves in the ground and continuously damage any enemy that touches them. They can be recalled by pressing ⚒ Use / Attack again, which will damage enemies along the way. They can also be recalled mid-throw. Their best modifier is Legendary.

name: Red Phaseblade
description: Phaseblades are a group of pre-Hardmode broadswords. There are nine different color variations with identical statistics. All phaseblades emit a small amount of light when swung. Letting go of ⚒ Use / Attack while swinging will throw the weapons, causing them to sharply drop down after travelling for 0.5 seconds[1] or upon contact with blocks, then embed themselves in the ground and continuously damage any enemy that touches them. They can be recalled by pressing ⚒ Use / Attack again, which will damage enemies along the way. They can also be recalled mid-throw. Phasesabers are the Hardmode upgrades of the phaseblades.

name: Red Phasesaber
description: Phasesabers are a group of Hardmode broadswords, direct upgrades to the phaseblades with much more damage and slightly faster use time. There are nine different color variations with identical statistics. All phasesabers emit a small amount of light when swung. Letting go of ⚒ Use / Attack while swinging will throw the weapons, causing them to sharply drop down after travelling for 0.5 seconds[1] or upon contact with blocks, then embed themselves in the ground and continuously damage any enemy that touches them. They can be recalled by pressing ⚒ Use / Attack again, which will damage enemies along the way. They can also be recalled mid-throw. Their best modifier is Legendary.

name: Rich Mahogany Sword
description: The Rich Mahogany Sword is a pre-Hardmode broadsword and a variation of the Wooden Sword. Its best modifier is Legendary.

name: Seedler
description: The Seedler is a Hardmode broadsword that has a 1/8 (12.5%) chance of being dropped by Plantera. When swung, it fires Seedler Nut projectiles that travel in a downwards arc before exploding into 4–7 Seedler Thorns upon hitting an enemy or after 0.33–2.15 seconds.[1] Seedler Thorns deal 70% of the weapon's damage (35 base damage) and knockback (base knockback of 4.2 (Average)),[2] are unaffected by gravity, and target nearby enemies when fired. Its best modifier is Legendary.

name: Shadewood Sword
description: The Shadewood Sword is a pre-Hardmode broadsword and a variation of the Wooden Sword. Its Corruption counterpart is the Ebonwood Sword.

name: Silver Broadsword
description: The Silver Broadsword is a pre-Hardmode broadsword. Its alternate ore counterpart is the Tungsten Broadsword.

name: Slap Hand
description: The Slap Hand is a Hardmode broadsword that can be purchased from the Skeleton Merchant for 25 GC during a Blood Moon. It is notable for having extremely high base knockback – a rating of 20, one of the highest values among all player-caused sources of knockback. The Slap Hand also has a 19% base critical strike chance, as opposed to the usual 4%. Its best modifier is Legendary.

name: Star Wrath
description: The Star Wrath is a Hardmode broadsword that has a 1/5 (20%) chance of being dropped by the Moon Lord. When swung, it causes three pink stars to fall toward the cursor. The stars can pierce one enemy and pass through blocks until they are at the cursor's altitude or less than 12.5 tiles above the player's center[1], after which they will dissipate upon contact with a block.[2] Its best modifier is Legendary.

stats.Tooltip: Causes stars to rain from the sky

name: Starfury
description: The Starfury is a pre-Hardmode broadsword that has a 1/4 (25%) chance of being found inside Skyware Chests on Floating Islands, as well as a 1/4 (25%) chance of being obtained from Sky Crates(Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) and Azure Crates(Desktop, Console and Mobile versions). When swung, it occasionally summons a star projectile that falls from the sky to the cursor. The star emits light, deals ×1.5 the weapon's damage (37 base damage) but no knockback, and pierces once.[1] The star will pass through all blocks until it reaches the lowest empty space at or above the cursor, after which it will break on the next block it hits. Its best modifier is Legendary.

stats.Tooltip: Causes stars to rain from the sky'Forged with the fury of heaven'

name: Stylish Scissors
description: The Stylish Scissors are a pre-Hardmode broadsword that have a 1/8 (12.5%) chance of being dropped by the Stylist. Letting go of ⚒ Use / Attack while swinging the weapon will throw the scissors in the direction of the cursor. The thrown scissors travel forward for 0.25 seconds before sharply dropping down, and can pierce through one enemy before dissipating on the second hit. The weapon and its projectiles both inflict the Bleeding debuff for 4-6 seconds on hit. Its best modifier is Legendary.

name: Tentacle Spike
description: The Tentacle Spike is a pre-Hardmode broadsword dropped by Eaters of Souls, Devourers, Crimeras, Face Monsters, and Blood Crawlers with a 1/525 (0.19%) chance. When swung, it sticks tentacle spikes into enemies on hit, which linger on the enemy and deal 3 damage per second for 9 seconds. The spikes can stack up to 5 times on each target.[1] On The Constant seed, the drop chance is increased to a 1/100 (1%) chance.

stats.Tooltip: Embeds damaging spikes in enemies'Never grab the pointy end.'

name: Terra Blade
description: The Terra Blade is a Hardmode, post-Plantera broadsword. When swung, a green aura follows the swing, reaching farther than the sword itself. The aura can hit up to three enemies. The sword will also fire a green slash projectile that pierces infinitely, dealing the weapon's damage but reducing its damage by 25% for each target it pierces.[1] The projectile will dissipate after 1.5 seconds,[2] upon contact with blocks, or when its damage falls to 0 after piercing.[3] Its best modifier is Legendary.

name: The Horseman's Blade
description: The Horseman's Blade is a Hardmode, post-Plantera broadsword that can be dropped by the Pumpking during the Pumpkin Moon event with a 2.5–12.5% (6.25–12.5%) chance depending on the current wave.[1] When swung, an orange aura follows the swing, reaching farther than the sword itself. The aura can hit up to three enemies. Upon striking an enemy, the Horseman's Blade summons a Flaming Jack, a homing pumpkin head projectile that can pass through blocks. Its best modifier is Legendary.

stats.Tooltip: Summons Pumpkin heads to attack your enemies

name: Tin Broadsword
description: The Tin Broadsword is a pre-Hardmode broadsword. Its alternate ore counterpart is the Copper Broadsword.

name: Titanium Sword
description: The Titanium Sword is a Hardmode broadsword. Its alternate ore counterpart is the Adamantite Sword.

name: True Excalibur
description: The True Excalibur is a Hardmode, post-mechanical bosses broadsword and the direct upgrade to the Excalibur. When swung, a large yellow aura with a pink edge follows the swing, reaching about twice the distance of the sword itself. The aura can hit up to six enemies. Its best modifier is Legendary.

name: True Night's Edge
description: The True Night's Edge is a Hardmode, post-mechanical bosses broadsword and the direct upgrade to the Night's Edge. When swung, a circular purple aura follows the swing, reaching farther than the sword itself. The aura can hit up to two enemies. The sword will also fire a spinning green blade that deals 50% of the weapon's damage (35 base damage)[1] and can strike up to 3 times. Its best modifier is Legendary.

name: Tungsten Broadsword
description: The Tungsten Broadsword is a pre-Hardmode broadsword. Its alternate ore counterpart is the Silver Broadsword.

name: Volcano
description: The Volcano is a pre-Hardmode broadsword. When swung, it creates an explosion upon enemy hits, dealing 75% of the weapon's damage (30 base damage) to the enemy and 2 other targets in an area around the enemy.[1] The Volcano has a 1/2 (50%) chance to inflict the On Fire! debuff for 3 seconds, while its explosion is guaranteed to inflict it for the same duration.[2] Its best modifier is Legendary.

stats.Tooltip: 'It's made out of fire!'

name: White Phaseblade
description: Phaseblades are a group of pre-Hardmode broadswords. There are nine different color variations with identical statistics. All phaseblades emit a small amount of light when swung. Letting go of ⚒ Use / Attack while swinging will throw the weapons, causing them to sharply drop down after travelling for 0.5 seconds[1] or upon contact with blocks, then embed themselves in the ground and continuously damage any enemy that touches them. They can be recalled by pressing ⚒ Use / Attack again, which will damage enemies along the way. They can also be recalled mid-throw. Phasesabers are the Hardmode upgrades of the phaseblades.

name: White Phasesaber
description: Phasesabers are a group of Hardmode broadswords, direct upgrades to the phaseblades with much more damage and slightly faster use time. There are nine different color variations with identical statistics. All phasesabers emit a small amount of light when swung. Letting go of ⚒ Use / Attack while swinging will throw the weapons, causing them to sharply drop down after travelling for 0.5 seconds[1] or upon contact with blocks, then embed themselves in the ground and continuously damage any enemy that touches them. They can be recalled by pressing ⚒ Use / Attack again, which will damage enemies along the way. They can also be recalled mid-throw. Their best modifier is Legendary.

name: Wooden Sword
description: The Wooden Sword is a pre-Hardmode broadsword. Its best modifier is Legendary.

name: Yellow Phaseblade
description: Phaseblades are a group of pre-Hardmode broadswords. There are nine different color variations with identical statistics. All phaseblades emit a small amount of light when swung. Letting go of ⚒ Use / Attack while swinging will throw the weapons, causing them to sharply drop down after travelling for 0.5 seconds[1] or upon contact with blocks, then embed themselves in the ground and continuously damage any enemy that touches them. They can be recalled by pressing ⚒ Use / Attack again, which will damage enemies along the way. They can also be recalled mid-throw. Phasesabers are the Hardmode upgrades of the phaseblades.

name: Yellow Phasesaber
description: Phasesabers are a group of Hardmode broadswords, direct upgrades to the phaseblades with much more damage and slightly faster use time. There are nine different color variations with identical statistics. All phasesabers emit a small amount of light when swung. Letting go of ⚒ Use / Attack while swinging will throw the weapons, causing them to sharply drop down after travelling for 0.5 seconds[1] or upon contact with blocks, then embed themselves in the ground and continuously damage any enemy that touches them. They can be recalled by pressing ⚒ Use / Attack again, which will damage enemies along the way. They can also be recalled mid-throw. Their best modifier is Legendary.

name: Zombie Arm
description: The Zombie Arm is a pre-Hardmode broadsword that has a 1/250 (0.4%) chance of being dropped by Zombies, Frozen Zombies, and Raincoat Zombies. When held, it visually appears on the player. It will also randomly release visual Zombie gore when swung. Its best modifier is Legendary.

name: Copper Shortsword
description: The Copper Shortsword is a pre-Hardmode shortsword. Newly created characters spawn with a Copper Shortsword (Iron Shortsword in Journey Mode). Its alternate ore counterpart is the Tin Shortsword.

name: Gladius
description: The Gladius is a pre-Hardmode shortsword that is dropped by Hoplites with a 1/20 (5%) chance. It attacks three times in quick succession per use, with slightly varying angles with each stab. Its best modifier is Godly or Demonic. Both modifiers increase the average damage output by the same amount.

stats.Tooltip: 'Are you not entertained?!'

name: Gold Shortsword
description: The Gold Shortsword is a pre-Hardmode shortsword. Its alternate ore counterpart is the Platinum Shortsword.

name: Iron Shortsword
description: The Iron Shortsword is a pre-Hardmode shortsword. In Journey Mode, the Iron Shortsword replaces the Copper Shortsword for newly created characters. Its alternate ore counterpart is the Lead Shortsword.

name: Lead Shortsword
description: The Lead Shortsword is a pre-Hardmode shortsword. Its alternate ore counterpart is the Iron Shortsword.

name: Platinum Shortsword
description: The Platinum Shortsword is a pre-Hardmode shortsword. Its alternate ore counterpart is the Gold Shortsword.

name: Ruler
description: The Ruler is a pre-Hardmode shortsword purchased from the Goblin Tinkerer for 10 SC. It swings four times in quick succession per use, with slightly varying angles with each stab. Its best modifier is Legendary.

name: Silver Shortsword
description: The Silver Shortsword is a pre-Hardmode shortsword. Its alternate ore counterpart is the Tungsten Shortsword.

name: Tin Shortsword
description: The Tin Shortsword is a pre-Hardmode shortsword. Its alternate ore counterpart is the Copper Shortsword.

name: Tragic Umbrella
description: The Tragic Umbrella is a pre-Hardmode shortsword and held item that can be purchased from the Clothier for 10 GC while in a Graveyard. It functions identical to the standard Umbrella, aside from dealing higher damage. While held, the Tragic Umbrella appears above the character, allowing them to fall at a slower rate than normal and preventing fall damage. The player can hold ▼ Down to increase the fall speed.

stats.Tooltip: You will fall slower while holding this

name: Tungsten Shortsword
description: The Tungsten Shortsword is a pre-Hardmode shortsword. Its alternate ore counterpart is the Silver Shortsword.

name: Umbrella
description: The Umbrella is a pre-Hardmode shortsword and held item. It can be found in surface Chests and can also be purchased for 1 GC from the Skeleton Merchant during a Waning Gibbous. When held, the Umbrella appears above the character, allowing the player to fall at a slower rate and preventing fall damage. The player can hold ▼ Down to increase the fall speed.

stats.Tooltip: You will fall slower while holding this
